     3	module LLM
     4	  ##
     5	  # The Bedrock class implements a provider for
     6	  # [Amazon Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/).
     7	  #
     8	  # Bedrock provides access to foundation models from Anthropic, Meta,
     9	  # Mistral, AI21 Labs, Cohere, and more through the AWS infrastructure.
    10	  # This provider uses the Bedrock Converse API for chat completions,
    11	  # and the Converse Stream API for streaming.
    12	  #
    13	  # Unlike other llm.rb providers which use API key authentication,
    14	  # Bedrock uses AWS Signature V4 (SigV4) for request signing.
    15	  # You must provide AWS credentials (access key, secret key, and region)
    16	  # instead of a single API key.
    17	  #
    18	  # Streaming uses the AWS Event Stream binary protocol instead of
    19	  # standard SSE. The binary framing is decoded inline using only
    20	  # Ruby's stdlib.
    21	  #
    22	  # @example
    23	  #   require "llm"
    24	  #
    25	  #   llm = LLM.bedrock(
    26	  #     access_key_id: ENV["AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"],
    27	  #     secret_access_key: ENV["AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"],
    28	  #     region: "us-east-1"
    29	  #   )
    30	  #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
    31	  #   ctx.talk "Hello, how are you?"
    32	  #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { puts _1.content }
    33	  class Bedrock < Provider
    34	    require_relative "bedrock/signature"
    35	    require_relative "bedrock/error_handler"
    36	    require_relative "bedrock/request_adapter"
    37	    require_relative "bedrock/request_adapter/completion"
    38	    require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter"
    39	    require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter/completion"
    40	    require_relative "bedrock/response_adapter/models"
    41	    require_relative "bedrock/stream_decoder"
    42	    require_relative "bedrock/stream_parser"
    43	    require_relative "bedrock/models"
    44	
    45	    include RequestAdapter
    46	
    47	    HOST_PATTERN = "bedrock-runtime.%s.amazonaws.com"
    48	
    49	    ##
    50	    # @param [String] access_key_id AWS access key ID
    51	    # @param [String] secret_access_key AWS secret access key
    52	    # @param [String] region AWS region (e.g. "us-east-1")
    53	    # @param [String, nil] session_token AWS session token for temporary credentials
    54	    # @param [String, nil] host Override the Bedrock API host
    55	    # @param [Integer] port Connection port
    56	    # @param [Boolean] ssl Whether to use SSL
    57	    # @param [Integer] timeout Request timeout in seconds
    58	    def initialize(access_key_id: nil, secret_access_key: nil,
    59	                   region: nil, session_token: nil,
    60	                   host: nil, port: 443, ssl: true, timeout: 60,
    61	                   **)
    62	      region ||= "us-east-1"
    63	      @access_key_id = access_key_id
    64	      @secret_access_key = secret_access_key
    65	      @aws_region = region
    66	      @session_token = session_token
    67	      host ||= HOST_PATTERN % region
    68	      @aws_host = host
    69	      super(key: @access_key_id, host:, port:, ssl:, timeout:, persistent: false)
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    ##
    73	    # @return [Symbol] Returns the provider's name
    74	    def name
    75	      :bedrock
    76	    end
    77	
    78	    ##
    79	    # Provides an interface to the Bedrock Converse API
    80	    #
    81	    # @see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/APIReference/API_runtime_Converse.html
    82	    #
    83	    # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    84	    # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    85	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    86	    def complete(prompt, params = {})
    87	      params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
    88	      req, messages, body = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, stream:)
    89	      tracer.set_request_metadata(user_input: extract_user_input(messages, fallback: prompt))
    90	      sign!(req, body)
    91	      model_id = model_id_for(req.path)
    92	      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream:, operation: "chat", stream_parser:, model: model_id)
    93	      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
    94	        .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
    95	      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: model_id, res:, span:)
    96	      res
    97	    end
    98	
    99	    ##
   100	    # Provides an interface to Bedrock's ListFoundationModels API.
   101	    #
   102	    # @note
   103	    #  Unlike the Converse API (bedrock-runtime), this endpoint lives
   104	    #  on the control plane (bedrock.<region>.amazonaws.com).
   105	    #
   106	    # @see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/APIReference/API_ListFoundationModels.html
   107	    # @return [LLM::Bedrock::Models]
   108	    def models
   109	      LLM::Bedrock::Models.new(self)
   110	    end
   111	
   112	    ##
   113	    # @raise [NotImplementedError]
   114	    def files
   115	      raise NotImplementedError
   116	    end
   117	
   118	    ##
   119	    # @raise [NotImplementedError]
   120	    def images
   121	      raise NotImplementedError
   122	    end
   123	
   124	    ##
   125	    # @raise [NotImplementedError]
   126	    def audio
   127	      raise NotImplementedError
   128	    end
   129	
   130	    ##
   131	    # @raise [NotImplementedError]
   132	    def moderations
   133	      raise NotImplementedError
   134	    end
   135	
   136	    ##
   137	    # @raise [NotImplementedError]
   138	    def responses
   139	      raise NotImplementedError
   140	    end
   141	
   142	    ##
   143	    # @raise [NotImplementedError]
   144	    def vector_stores
   145	      raise NotImplementedError
   146	    end
   147	
   148	    ##
   149	    # @raise [NotImplementedError]
   150	    def embed(input, model: nil, **params)
   151	      raise NotImplementedError
   152	    end
   153	
   154	    ##
   155	    # @return [String]
   156	    def assistant_role
   157	      "assistant"
   158	    end
   159	
   160	    ##
   161	    # Bedrock expects tool results as user messages containing
   162	    # `toolResult` content blocks rather than a distinct `tool` role.
   163	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#tool_role)
   164	    def tool_role
   165	      :user
   166	    end
   167	
   168	    ##
   169	    # @return [String]
   170	    def default_model
   171	      "deepseek.v3.2"
   172	    end
   173	
   174	    private
   175	
   176	    def headers
   177	      lock do
   178	        (@headers || {}).merge("Content-Type" => "application/json")
   179	      end
   180	    end
   181	
   182	    def credentials
   183	      LLM::Object.from(
   184	        access_key_id: @access_key_id,
   185	        secret_access_key: @secret_access_key,
   186	        aws_region: @aws_region,
   187	        host: @aws_host,
   188	        session_token: @session_token
   189	      )
   190	    end
   191	
   192	    def stream_parser
   193	      LLM::Bedrock::StreamParser
   194	    end
   195	
   196	    def error_handler
   197	      LLM::Bedrock::ErrorHandler
   198	    end
   199	
   200	    def stream_decoder
   201	      LLM::Bedrock::StreamDecoder
   202	    end
   203	
   204	    def normalize_complete_params(params)
   205	      params = {role: :user, model: default_model, max_tokens: 2048}.merge!(params)
   206	      tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
   207	      params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
   208	      role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
   209	      params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
   210	      [params, stream, tools, role]
   211	    end
   212	
   213	    def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, stream: nil)
   214	      messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   215	      model_id = params.delete(:model) || default_model
   216	      payload = build_converse_payload(messages, params)
   217	      body = LLM.json.dump(payload)
   218	      path = stream ? "/model/#{model_id}/converse-stream" \
   219	                    : "/model/#{model_id}/converse"
   220	      req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
   221	      transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
   222	      [req, messages, body]
   223	    end
   224	
   225	    def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   226	      if LLM::Prompt === prompt
   227	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt]
   228	      else
   229	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
   230	      end
   231	    end
   232	
   233	    def build_converse_payload(messages, params)
   234	      adapted = adapt(messages)
   235	      payload = {}
   236	      payload[:system] = adapted[:system] if adapted[:system]&.any?
   237	      payload[:messages] = adapted[:messages]
   238	      inference_config = {}
   239	      inference_config[:maxTokens] = params.delete(:max_tokens) if params[:max_tokens]
   240	      inference_config[:temperature] = params.delete(:temperature) if params.key?(:temperature)
   241	      inference_config[:topP] = params.delete(:top_p) if params.key?(:top_p)
   242	      inference_config[:stopSequences] = params.delete(:stop) if params[:stop]
   243	      payload[:inferenceConfig] = inference_config unless inference_config.empty?
   244	      payload[:toolConfig] = params.delete(:toolConfig) if params[:toolConfig]
   245	      payload[:outputConfig] = params.delete(:outputConfig) if params[:outputConfig]
   246	      additional = {}
   247	      top_k = params.delete(:top_k)
   248	      additional[:top_k] = top_k if top_k
   249	      payload[:additionalModelRequestFields] = additional unless additional.empty?
   250	      payload
   251	    end
   252	
   253	    def extract_user_input(messages, fallback:)
   254	      message = messages.reverse.find(&:user?) || messages.last
   255	      value = message&.content || fallback
   256	      value.is_a?(String) ? value : LLM.json.dump(value)
   257	    end
   258	
   259	    def model_id_for(path)
   260	      path[%r{\A/model/(.+?)/converse(?:-stream)?\z}, 1] || default_model
   261	    end
   262	
   263	    def sign!(req, body)
   264	      Signature.new(
   265	        credentials:,
   266	        method: req.method,
   267	        path: req.path,
   268	        body:
   269	      ).sign!(req)
   270	    end
   271	  end
   272	end
===== lib/llm/providers/ollama.rb =====
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module LLM
     4	  ##
     5	  # The Ollama class implements a provider for [Ollama](https://ollama.ai/) –
     6	  # and the provider supports a wide range of models. It is straight forward
     7	  # to run on your own hardware, and there are a number of multi-modal models
     8	  # that can process both images and text.
     9	  #
    10	  # @example
    11	  #   #!/usr/bin/env ruby
    12	  #   require "llm"
    13	  #
    14	  #   llm = LLM.ollama(key: nil)
    15	  #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm, model: "llava")
    16	  #   ctx.talk ["Tell me about this image", ctx.local_file("/images/photo.png")]
    17	  #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { print "[#{_1.role}]", _1.content, "\n" }
    18	  class Ollama < Provider
    19	    require_relative "ollama/error_handler"
    20	    require_relative "ollama/request_adapter"
    21	    require_relative "ollama/response_adapter"
    22	    require_relative "ollama/stream_parser"
    23	    require_relative "ollama/models"
    24	
    25	    include RequestAdapter
    26	
    27	    HOST = "localhost"
    28	
    29	    ##
    30	    # @param key (see LLM::Provider#initialize)
    31	    def initialize(**)
    32	      super(host: HOST, port: 11434, ssl: false, **)
    33	    end
    34	
    35	    ##
    36	    # @return [Symbol]
    37	    #  Returns the provider's name
    38	    def name
    39	      :ollama
    40	    end
    41	
    42	    ##
    43	    # Provides an embedding
    44	    # @param input (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    45	    # @param model (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    46	    # @param params (see LLM::Provider#embed)
    47	    # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
    48	    # @return [LLM::Response]
    49	    def embed(input, model: default_model, **params)
    50	      params   = {model:}.merge!(params)
    51	      req      = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/v1/embeddings", headers)
    52	      req.body = LLM.json.dump({input:}.merge!(params))
    53	      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
    54	      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
    55	      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)
    56	      res
    57	    end
    58	
    59	    ##
    60	    # Provides an interface to the chat completions API
    61	    # @see https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#generate-a-chat-completion Ollama docs
    62	    # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    63	    # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    64	    # @example (see LLM::Provider#complete)
    65	    # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)
    66	    # @raise [LLM::PromptError]
    67	    #  When given an object a provider does not understand
    68	    # @return [LLM::Response]
    69	    def complete(prompt, params = {})
    70	      params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
    71	      req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
    72	      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
    73	      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
    74	        .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
    75	      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
    76	      res
    77	    end
    78	
    79	    ##
    80	    # Provides an interface to Ollama's models API
    81	    # @see https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#list-local-models Ollama docs
    82	    # @return [LLM::Ollama::Models]
    83	    def models
    84	      LLM::Ollama::Models.new(self)
    85	    end
    86	
    87	    ##
    88	    # @return (see LLM::Provider#assistant_role)
    89	    def assistant_role
    90	      "assistant"
    91	    end
    92	
    93	    ##
    94	    # Returns the default model for chat completions
    95	    # @see https://ollama.com/library/qwen3 qwen3
    96	    # @return [String]
    97	    def default_model
    98	      "qwen3:latest"
    99	    end
   100	
   101	    private
   102	
   103	    def headers
   104	      lock do
   105	        (@headers || {}).merge(
   106	          "Content-Type" => "application/json",
   107	          "Authorization" => "Bearer #{@key}"
   108	        )
   109	      end
   110	    end
   111	
   112	    def stream_parser
   113	      LLM::Ollama::StreamParser
   114	    end
   115	
   116	    def error_handler
   117	      LLM::Ollama::ErrorHandler
   118	    end
   119	
   120	    def normalize_complete_params(params)
   121	      params = {role: :user, model: default_model, stream: true}.merge!(params)
   122	      tools  = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
   123	      params = [params, {format: params[:schema]}, adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
   124	      role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
   125	      params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
   126	      [params, stream, tools, role]
   127	    end
   128	
   129	    def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
   130	      messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   131	      body = LLM.json.dump({messages: [adapt(messages)].flatten}.merge!(params))
   132	      req = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/api/chat", headers)
   133	      transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
   134	      req
   135	    end
   136	
   137	    def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
   138	      if LLM::Prompt === prompt
   139	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt.to_a]
   140	      else
   141	        [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
   142	      end
   143	    end
   144	  end
   145	end

===== contract.rb =====
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module LLM
     4	  ##
     5	  # The `LLM::Contract` module provides the ability for modules
     6	  # who are extended by it to implement contracts which must be
     7	  # implemented by other modules who include a given contract.
     8	  #
     9	  # @example
    10	  #   module LLM::Contract
    11	  #      # ..
    12	  #   end
    13	  #
    14	  #   module LLM::Contract
    15	  #     module Completion
    16	  #       extend LLM::Contract
    17	  #       # inheriting modules must implement these methods
    18	  #       # otherwise an error is raised on include
    19	  #       def foo = nil
    20	  #       def bar = nil
    21	  #     end
    22	  #   end
    23	  #
    24	  #   module LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter
    25	  #     module Completion
    26	  #       def foo = nil
    27	  #       def bar = nil
    28	  #       include LLM::Contract::Completion
    29	  #     end
    30	  #   end
    31	  module Contract
    32	    ContractError = Class.new(LLM::Error)
    33	    require_relative "contract/completion"
    34	
    35	    ##
    36	    # @api private
    37	    def included(mod)
    38	      meths = mod.instance_methods(false)
    39	      if meths.empty?
    40	        raise ContractError, "#{mod} does not implement any methods required by #{self}"
    41	      end
    42	      missing = instance_methods - meths
    43	      if missing.any?

     1	# frozen_string_literal: true

     3	module LLM
     4	  ##
     5	  # The `LLM::Contract` module provides the ability for modules
     6	  # who are extended by it to implement contracts which must be
     7	  # implemented by other modules who include a given contract.
     8	  #
     9	  # @example
    10	  #   module LLM::Contract
    11	  #      # ..
    12	  #   end
    13	  #
    14	  #   module LLM::Contract
    15	  #     module Completion
    16	  #       extend LLM::Contract
    17	  #       # inheriting modules must implement these methods
    18	  #       # otherwise an error is raised on include
    19	  #       def foo = nil
    20	  #       def bar = nil
    21	  #     end
    22	  #   end
    23	  #
    24	  #   module LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter
    25	  #     module Completion
    26	  #       def foo = nil
    27	  #       def bar = nil
    28	  #       include LLM::Contract::Completion
    29	  #     end
    30	  #   end
    31	  module Contract
    32	    ContractError = Class.new(LLM::Error)
    33	    require_relative "contract/completion"
    34	
    35	    ##
    36	    # @api private
    37	    def included(mod)
    38	      meths = mod.instance_methods(false)
    39	      if meths.empty?
    40	        raise ContractError, "#{mod} does not implement any methods required by #{self}"
    41	      end
    42	      missing = instance_methods - meths
    43	      if missing.any?
    44	        raise ContractError, "#{mod} does not implement methods (#{missing.join(", ")}) required by #{self}"
    45	      end
    46	    end
    47	  end
    48	end

===== transport.rb =====
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module LLM
     4	  ##
     5	  # The {LLM::Transport LLM::Transport} class defines the execution
     6	  # interface used by {LLM::Provider}.
     7	  #
     8	  # Custom transports can subclass this class and override {#request} to
     9	  # execute provider requests without changing request adapters or
    10	  # response adapters.
    11	  #
    12	  # Providers should construct {LLM::Transport::Request} objects before
    13	  # delegating to a transport. Custom transports can execute those
    14	  # requests directly, or transform them into backend-specific request
    15	  # objects before execution.
    16	  #
    17	  # Only {#request} is required. The remaining methods are optional hooks
    18	  # for features such as interruption, request ownership, or persistence,
    19	  # and only need to be implemented when the underlying adapter can
    20	  # support them.
    21	  #
    22	  # Returned responses should implement the
    23	  # {LLM::Transport::Response LLM::Transport::Response} interface. In
    24	  # practice this can mean adapting another client's response object so
    25	  # existing provider execution, response adapters, and error handlers
    26	  # can rely on one normalized response contract instead of
    27	  # transport-specific classes.
    28	  class Transport
    29	    require_relative "transport/request"
    30	    require_relative "transport/response"
    31	    require_relative "transport/utils"
    32	    require_relative "transport/stream_decoder"
    33	    require_relative "transport/net_http_adapter"
    34	    require_relative "transport/http"
    35	    require_relative "transport/persistent_http"
    36	    require_relative "transport/curb"
    37	    require_relative "transport/execution"
    38	
    39	    ##
    40	    # Returns the built-in Net::HTTP transport class.
    41	    # @return [Class]
    42	    def self.net_http
    43	      HTTP
    44	    end
    45	
    46	    ##
    47	    # Returns the built-in Net::HTTP::Persistent transport class.
    48	    # @return [Class]
    49	    def self.net_http_persistent
    50	      PersistentHTTP
    51	    end
    52	
    53	    ##
    54	    # Returns the optional libcurl (curb) transport class.
    55	    # Requires the `curb` gem.
    56	    # @return [Class]
    57	    def self.curb
    58	      Curb
    59	    end
    60	
    61	    ##
    62	    # Performs a request through the transport.
    63	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
    64	    # @param [Object] owner
    65	    # @param [LLM::Object, nil] stream
    66	    # @yieldparam [LLM::Transport::Response] response
    67	    # @return [Object]
    68	    def request(request, owner:, stream: nil, &)
    69	      raise NotImplementedError
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    ##
    73	    # Returns the current request owner.
    74	    # @return [Object]
    75	    def request_owner
    76	      return Fiber.current unless defined?(::Async)
    77	      Async::Task.current? ? Async::Task.current : Fiber.current
    78	    end
    79	
    80	    ##
    81	    # Returns the exception classes that indicate an interrupted request.
    82	    # @return [Array<Class<Exception>>]
    83	    def interrupt_errors
    84	      []
    85	    end
    86	
    87	    ##
    88	    # Interrupt an active request, if any.
    89	    # @param [Object] owner
    90	    # @return [nil]
    91	    def interrupt!(owner)
    92	      raise NotImplementedError
    93	    end
    94	
    95	    ##
    96	    # Returns whether an execution owner was interrupted.
    97	    # @param [Object] owner
    98	    # @return [Boolean, nil]
    99	    def interrupted?(owner)
   100	      nil
   101	    end
   102	
   103	    ##
   104	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
   105	    # @param [IO] io
   106	    # @return [void]
   107	    def set_body_stream(request, io)
   108	      request.body_stream = io
   109	      request["transfer-encoding"] = "chunked" unless request["content-length"]
   110	    end
   111	  end
   112	end

content.content:
===== transport/execution.rb =====
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	class LLM::Transport
     4	  ##
     5	  # Internal request execution methods for {LLM::Provider}.
     6	  #
     7	  # This module handles provider-side transport execution, response
     8	  # parsing, streaming, and request body setup.
     9	  #
    10	  # @api private
    11	  module Execution
    12	    private
    13	
    14	    ##
    15	    # Executes a HTTP request
    16	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
    17	    #  The request to send
    18	    # @param [Proc] b
    19	    #  A block to yield the response to (optional)
    20	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
    21	    #  The response from the server
    22	    # @raise [LLM::Error::Unauthorized]
    23	    #  When authentication fails
    24	    # @raise [LLM::Error::RateLimit]
    25	    #  When the rate limit is exceeded
    26	    # @raise [LLM::Error]
    27	    #  When any other unsuccessful status code is returned
    28	    # @raise [SystemCallError]
    29	    #  When there is a network error at the operating system level
    30	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
    31	    def execute(request:, operation:, stream: nil, stream_parser: self.stream_parser, model: nil, inputs: nil, &b)
    32	      stream &&= LLM::Object.from(streamer: stream, parser: stream_parser, decoder: stream_decoder)
    33	      owner = transport.request_owner
    34	      tracer = self.tracer
    35	      span = tracer.on_request_start(operation:, model:, inputs:)
    36	      res = transport.request(request, owner:, stream:, &b)
    37	      res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
    38	      [handle_response(res, tracer, span), span, tracer]
    39	    rescue *transport.interrupt_errors
    40	      raise LLM::Interrupt, "request interrupted" if transport.interrupted?(owner)
    41	      raise
    42	    end
    43	
    44	    ##
    45	    # Handles the response from a request
    46	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
    47	    #  The response to handle
    48	    # @param [Object, nil] span
    49	    #  The span
    50	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
    51	    def handle_response(res, tracer, span)
    52	      res.ok? ? res.body = parse_response(res) : error_handler.new(tracer, span, res).raise_error!
    53	      res
    54	    end
    55	
    56	    ##
    57	    # Parse a HTTP response
    58	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
    59	    # @return [LLM::Object, String]
    60	    def parse_response(res)
    61	      case res["content-type"]
    62	      when %r{\Aapplication/json\s*} then LLM::Object.from(LLM.json.load(res.body))
    63	      else res.body
    64	      end
    65	    end
    66	  end
    67	end

    50	    # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
    51	    def handle_response(res, tracer, span)
    52	      res.ok? ? res.body = parse_response(res) : error_handler.new(tracer, span, res).raise_error!
    53	      res
    54	    end
    55	
    56	    ##
    57	    # Parse a HTTP response
    58	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
    59	    # @return [LLM::Object, String]
    60	    def parse_response(res)
    61	      case res["content-type"]
    62	      when %r{\Aapplication/json\s*} then LLM::Object.from(LLM.json.load(res.body))
    63	      else res.body
    64	      end
    65	    end
    66	  end
    67	end

    24	    def adapt(res, type:)
    25	      response = (LLM::Response === res) ? res : LLM::Response.new(res)

    34	      when :audio then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Audio
    35	      when :completion then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Completion
    36	      when :embedding then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::Embedding

    43	      when :web_search then LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter::WebSearch
    44	      else

    47	    end
    48	  end

content.content:
===== context talk/respond/complete =====
    # @example
    #   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])
    #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
    #   res = ctx.talk("Hello, what is your name?")
    #   puts res.messages[0].content
    def talk(prompt, params = {})
      @owner = @llm.request_owner
      compactor.compact!(prompt) if compactor.compact?(prompt)
      repair!(@messages, prompt)
      prompt, params, res = mode == :responses ? respond(prompt, params) : complete(prompt, params)
      self.compacted = false
      role = params[:role] || @llm.user_role
      role = @llm.tool_role if params[:role].nil? && [*prompt].grep(LLM::Function::Return).any?
      @messages.concat LLM::Prompt === prompt ? prompt.to_a : [LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
      @messages.concat [res.choices[-1]]
      res
    end

    ##
    # Ask a question and return the content string directly.
    # Accepts `with:` for file attachments and a block for streaming.
    # This interface is compatible with RubyLLM's `ask` method.
    # @param [String] prompt
      stream.on_transform_finish(self, transformer) if LLM::Stream === stream
    end

    ##
    # Executes a turn through the Responses API.
    # @api private
    def respond(prompt, params)
      params = @params.merge(params)
      prompt, params = transform(prompt, params)
      bind!(params[:stream], params[:model], params[:tools])
      res_id = params[:store] == false ? nil : @messages.find(&:assistant?)&.response&.response_id
      params = params.merge(previous_response_id: res_id, input: @messages.to_a).compact
      [prompt, params, @llm.responses.create(prompt, params)]
    end

    ##
    # Executes a turn through the chat completions API.
    # @api private
    def complete(prompt, params)
      params = params.merge(messages: @messages.to_a)
      params = @params.merge(params)
      prompt, params = transform(prompt, params)
      bind!(params[:stream], params[:model], params[:tools])
      [prompt, params, @llm.complete(prompt, params)]
    end

    ##
===== openai response_adapter/completion.rb =====
     1	# frozen_string_literal: true
     2	
     3	module LLM::OpenAI::ResponseAdapter
     4	  module Completion
     5	    ##
     6	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#messages)
     7	    def messages
     8	      body.choices.map.with_index do |choice, index|
     9	        message = choice.message
    10	        extra = {
    11	          index:, response: self,
    12	          logprobs: choice.logprobs,
    13	          reasoning_content: message.reasoning_content,
    14	          tool_calls: adapt_tool_calls(message.tool_calls),
    15	          original_tool_calls: message.tool_calls
    16	        }
    17	        LLM::Message.new(message.role, message.content, extra)
    18	      end
    19	    end
    20	    alias_method :choices, :messages
    21	
    22	    ##
    23	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_tokens)
    24	    def input_tokens
    25	      body.usage&.prompt_tokens || 0
    26	    end
    27	
    28	    ##
    29	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#output_tokens)
    30	    def output_tokens
    31	      body.usage&.completion_tokens || 0
    32	    end
    33	
    34	    ##
    35	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#reasoning_tokens)

    37	      body
    38	        .usage

    40	        &.reasoning_tokens || 0
    41	    end
    42

    50	    end
    51

    56	        .usage
    57	        &.completion_tokens_details
    58	        &.audio_tokens || 0
    59	    end
    60	
    61	    ##
    62	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#input_image_tokens)
    63	    def input_image_tokens
    64	      body
    65	        .usage
    66	        &.prompt_tokens_details
    67	        &.image_tokens || 0
    68	    end
    69	
    70	    ##
    71	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#cache_read_tokens)
    72	    def cache_read_tokens
    73	      body
    74	        .usage
    75	        &.prompt_tokens_details
    76	        &.cached_tokens || 0
    77	    end
    78	
    79	    ##
    80	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#cache_write_tokens)
    81	    def cache_write_tokens
    82	      0
    83	    end
    84	
    85	    ##
    86	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#total_tokens)
    87	    def total_tokens
    88	      body.usage&.total_tokens || 0
    89	    end
    90	
    91	    ##
    92	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#usage)
    93	    def usage
    94	      super
    95	    end
    96	
    97	    ##
    98	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#model)
    99	    def model
   100	      body.model
   101	    end
   102	
   103	    ##
   104	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#content)
   105	    def content
   106	      super
   107	    end
   108	
   109	    ##
   110	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#reasoning_content)
   111	    def reasoning_content
   112	      super
   113	    end
   114

   116	    # (see LLM::Contract::Completion#content!)
   117	    def content!

   119	    end

   121	    private
   122	
   123	    def adapt_tool_calls(tools)
   124	      (tools || []).filter_map do |tool|
   125	        next unless tool.function
   126	        {id: tool.id, name: tool.function.name, arguments: parse_tool_arguments(tool.function.arguments)}
   127	      end
   128	    end
   129	
   130	    def parse_tool_arguments(arguments)
   131	      return {} if arguments.to_s.empty?
   132	      parsed = LLM.json.load(arguments)
   133	      Hash === parsed ? parsed : {}
   134	    rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
   135	      {}
   136	    end
   137	
   138	    include LLM::Contract::Completion
   139	  end
   140	end

     4	  ##

     7	    ##

    11	
    12	    ##

    16

    20	    # @param [Object, nil] span
    21	    #  The span
    22	    # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
    23	    #  The response from the server
    24	    # @return [LLM::OpenAI::ErrorHandler]
    25	    def initialize(tracer, span, res)
    26	      @tracer = tracer
    27	      @span = span
    28	      @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
    29	    end
    30	
    31	    ##
    32	    # @raise [LLM::Error]
    33	    #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
    34	    def raise_error!
    35	      ex = error
    36	      @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
    37	    ensure
    38	      raise(ex) if ex
    39	    end
    40	
    41	    private
    42	
    43	    ##
    44	    # @return [String, LLM::Object]
    45	    def body
    46	      @body ||= parse_body!
    47	    end
    48	
    49	    ##
    50	    # @return [LLM::Error]
    51	    def error
    52	      if res.server_error?
    53	        LLM::ServerError.new("Server error").tap { _1.response = res }
    54	      elsif res.unauthorized?
    55	        LLM::UnauthorizedError.new("Authentication error").tap { _1.response = res }
    56	      elsif res.rate_limited?
    57	        LLM::RateLimitError.new("Too many requests").tap { _1.response = res }
    58	      elsif res.not_found?
    59	        LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
    60	      else
    61	        error = body["error"] || {}
    62	        case error["type"]
    63	        when "invalid_request_error" then handle_invalid_request(error)
    64	        when "server_error"
    65	          LLM::ServerError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
    66	        else
    67	          LLM::Error.new(error["message"] || "Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
    68	        end
    69	      end
    70	    end
    71	
    72	    ##
    73	    # @param [Exception] error
    74	    # @return [LLM::Error]
    75	    def handle_invalid_request(error)
    76	      case error["code"]
    77	      when "context_length_exceeded"
    78	        LLM::ContextWindowError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
    79	      else
    80	        LLM::InvalidRequestError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
    81	      end
    82	    end
    83	
    84	    ##
    85	    # Tries to parse the response body as a LLM::Object
    86	    # @return [String, LLM::Object]
    87	    def parse_body!
    88	      if String === res.body
    89	        LLM::Object.from LLM.json.load(res.body)
    90	      elsif Hash === res.body
    91	        LLM::Object.from(res.body)
    92	      else

    97	    end

     4	# The {LLM::Registry LLM::Registry} class provides a small API over
     5	# provider model data. It exposes model metadata such as pricing,
     6	# capabilities, modalities, and limits from the registry files
     7	# stored under `data/`. The data is provided by https://models.dev
     8	# and shipped with llm.rb.
     9	class LLM::Registry
    10	  @root = File.join(__dir__, "..", "..")
    11

    14	  #  Might raise an error
    15	  # @param [Symbol]

    17	  # @return [LLM::Registry]
    18	  def self.for(name)
    19	    path = File.join @root, "data", "#{name}.json"
    20	    if File.file?(path)
    21	      new LLM.json.load(File.binread(path))
    22	    else
    23	      raise LLM::NoSuchRegistryError, "no registry found for #{name}"
    24	    end
    25	  end
    26

require "setup"

RSpec.describe "LLM::Deepseek::Models" do
  let(:key) { ENV["DEEPSEEK_SECRET"] || "TOKEN" }
  let(:provider) { LLM.deepseek(key:) }

  describe "#default_model" do
    it "returns deepseek-v4-flash" do
      expect(provider.default_model).to eq("deepseek-v4-flash")
    end
  end

  context "when given a successful list operation",
          vcr: {cassette_name: "deepseek/models/successful_list"} do
    subject(:response) { provider.models.all }

    it "is successful" do
      is_expected.to be_instance_of(LLM::Response)
    end

    include_examples "LLM::Models contract"

    it "marks listed models as supporting chat" do
      expect(response.models.all?(&:chat?)).to be(true)
    end

content.content:
===== Rakefile 60-90 =====

namespace :'models.dev' do
  desc "Download models.dev metadata"
  task :download do
    require "net/http"
    require "json"
    client = Net::HTTP.new "models.dev", 443
    client.use_ssl = true
    res = client.request Net::HTTP::Get.new("/api.json")
    case res
    when Net::HTTPOK
      models = JSON.parse(res.body)
      providers = %w[openai google anthropic xai zai deepseek].to_h { [_1, _1] }
      providers["bedrock"] = "amazon-bedrock"
      providers.each do |target, source|
        File.binwrite "data/#{target}.json", JSON.pretty_generate(models[source])
      end
    else
      warn("error: #{res.class}")
      exit 1
    end
  end
end

namespace :agents do
  desc "Run the release agent"
  task :release do
    sh "bundle exec ruby agents/release-agent/main.rb"
  end
end

content.text:
I now have a complete picture across all seven steps. Here is the full map.

---

## Step 1 — Orientation

**What it is:** `llm.rb` — a single-runtime Ruby gem (`module LLM`) for talking to many LLM providers through one interface (chat/completions, embeddings, streaming, tools, plus agents, MCP, A2A, ActiveRecord/Sequel persistence). Pure-stdlib HTTP; optional deps loaded lazily.

**Layout (all under `lib/llm/`):**
- Entry/core: `lib/llm.rb` (the `LLM` module + provider factory methods), `provider.rb` (abstract base), `context.rb`, `message.rb`, `response.rb`, `prompt.rb`, `buffer.rb`.
- Cross-cutting machinery: `transport.rb` + `transport/` (HTTP, request/response, streaming decode, **`transport/execution.rb`** = the shared execute/parse loop), `contract.rb` + `contract/completion.rb` (the response interface), `error.rb` (typed errors), `registry.rb` (model metadata from `data/*.json`), `schema.rb`, `tracer/`, `stream/`, `usage.rb`, `cost.rb`.
- **Providers: `lib/llm/providers/`** — one `<name>.rb` class per provider plus a same-named directory of components. Providers: `openai`, `anthropic`, `google`, `ollama`, `bedrock` (full, subclass `LLM::Provider`); `deepseek`, `xai`, `zai`, `llamacpp` (thin, subclass `LLM::OpenAI`).

**How a caller obtains a provider:** module functions in `lib/llm.rb:112-181` (e.g. `LLM.openai` `lib/llm.rb:152-155`) — each lazily `require_relative`s the provider file then `.new(**)`. Providers are *not* eagerly required in `lib/llm.rb:6-39`.

---

## Step 2 — Trace of a chat call (entry → request → parsed result)

Order of execution for `LLM.openai(key:).chat("hi")`:

1. `LLM.openai(**)` — `lib/llm.rb:152-155` — lazy-require + `LLM::OpenAI.new`.
2. `LLM::Provider#chat` — `lib/llm/provider.rb:103-106` — builds `LLM::Context.new(self, params).talk(prompt, role:)`.
3. `LLM::Context#talk` — `lib/llm/context.rb:193-204` — dispatches to `complete` (`:204`→ line 197) (or `respond` for the Responses API).
4. `LLM::Context#complete` — `lib/llm/context.rb:558-564` — merges params/messages, then `@llm.complete(prompt, params)` at `:563`.
5. Provider `#complete` (concrete; base is abstract at `provider.rb:94-96`), e.g. `LLM::OpenAI#complete` — `lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:73-82`:
   - `normalize_complete_params` — `openai.rb:210-220` (defaults, tools, schema, stream flag).
   - `build_complete_request` — `openai.rb:222-228` → calls `RequestAdapter#adapt` (`openai/request_adapter.rb:17-25`) to shape messages, dumps JSON, builds `LLM::Transport::Request.post(completions_path, headers)`.
   - `execute(...)` (shared) — `lib/llm/transport/execution.rb:31-42` → `transport.request` → `handle_response` (`:51-54`) → `parse_response` (`:60-65`, JSON→`LLM::Object`).
   - `ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)` — `openai.rb:78` → `openai/response_adapter.rb:24-28,32-47` extends the `LLM::Response` with `ResponseAdapter::Completion`.
6. Parsed result: `openai/response_adapter/completion.rb:1-139`, which `include LLM::Contract::Completion` (`:138`) — exposes `messages/choices`, token counts, `content`. `Context#talk` appends `res.choices[-1]` to history (`context.rb:202`).

---

## Step 3 — Provider family & base contract

**Base:** `class LLM::Provider` (`lib/llm/provider.rb:8`), `include LLM::Transport::Execution` (`:9`). Subclasses: `Anthropic`, `Bedrock`, `Google`, `Ollama`, `OpenAI` extend it directly; `DeepSeek`, `XAI`, `ZAI`, `LlamaCpp` extend `LLM::OpenAI`.

**Abstract methods a provider must/may implement** (all raise `NotImplementedError` in base):
- `name` `:55`, `embed` `:70`, `complete` `:94`, `responses` `:126`, `images` `:133`, `audio` `:140`, `files` `:147`, `models` `:154`, `moderations` `:161`, `vector_stores` `:168`, `assistant_role` `:176`, `default_model` `:183`, `web_search` `:243`.
- Private hooks: `headers` `:362`, `error_handler` `:371`, `stream_parser` `:384` (must override); `event_handler` `:377` and `stream_decoder` `:391` have defaults.
- Provided defaults: `chat` `:103`, `respond` `:114`, `schema` `:190`, `with` `:204`, `server_tools` `:216`, `server_tool` `:233`, role accessors `user_role/system_role/developer_role/tool_role` `:249-269`, tracer methods `:274-316`.

**Per-provider customization:**
- **OpenAI** (`openai.rb:16`) — `include RequestAdapter`; implements `embed/complete/responses/images/audio/files/models/moderations/vector_stores/web_search/server_tools`; `assistant_role` `"assistant"`; default model `gpt-4.1`; private `completions_path` `:189-191`.
- **Anthropic** (`anthropic.rb:16`) — `include RequestAdapter`, `extend Utils`; no `embed`; `tool_role` overridden to `:user` (`:89-91`); `server_tools` bash/web_search/text_editor; request to `/v1/messages`.
- **Google** (`google.rb:20`) — overrides `user_role/system_role/developer_role` all to `:user` (`:154-170`), `assistant_role` `"model"`; key in URL query; `complete` returns `model` separately (`:77-85`).
- **Ollama** (`ollama.rb:18`) — `embed`+`complete`; streaming on by default (`:121`); `format: schema` instead of response_format.
- **Bedrock** (`bedrock.rb:33`) — AWS SigV4 instead of API key (`initialize` takes `access_key_id/secret_access_key/region`, `:58-70`); `sign!` `:263-270`; Converse payload builder `:233-251`; overrides `stream_decoder` `:200-202`; `tool_role :user`; no `embed/files/images/audio/moderations/responses/vector_stores`.
- **Reuse-by-subclassing of OpenAI:**
  - `DeepSeek` (`deepseek.rb:20`) — `include DeepSeek::RequestAdapter` (own request shaping), reuses OpenAI response/stream/error; disables `files/images/audio/moderations/responses/vector_stores`.
  - `XAI` (`xai.rb:17`) — adds own `images` (`XAI::Images`), disables others.
  - `ZAI` (`zai.rb:16`) — overrides private `completions_path` `:77-79`; disables others.
  - `LlamaCpp` (`llamacpp.rb:22`) — localhost/no-SSL defaults; disables others. Reuses *all* OpenAI components.

---

## Step 4 — Per-provider component families

Each full provider `require_relative`s its components at the top of its class. Families:

| Component | OpenAI | Anthropic | Google | Ollama | Bedrock |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **RequestAdapter** (`adapt`, `adapt_tools`, `adapt_schema`) | `openai/request_adapter.rb` + `request_adapter/{completion,respond,moderation}.rb` | `anthropic/request_adapter.rb` + `request_adapter/completion.rb` | `google/request_adapter.rb` + `request_adapter/*` | `ollama/request_adapter.rb` (+ completion) | `bedrock/request_adapter.rb` + `request_adapter/completion.rb` |
| **ResponseAdapter** (`adapt`/`select`) | `openai/response_adapter.rb` + 10 sub-adapters (`audio,completion,embedding,enumerable,file,image,models,moderations,responds,web_search`) | `anthropic/response_adapter.rb` + subs | `google/response_adapter.rb` + subs | `ollama/response_adapter.rb` | `bedrock/response_adapter.rb` + `response_adapter/{completion,models}.rb` |
| **error_handler** | `openai/error_handler.rb` | `anthropic/error_handler.rb` | `google/error_handler.rb` | `ollama/error_handler.rb` | `bedrock/error_handler.rb` |
| **stream_parser** | `openai/stream_parser.rb` | `anthropic/stream_parser.rb` | `google/stream_parser.rb` | `ollama/stream_parser.rb` | `bedrock/stream_parser.rb` |
| **stream_decoder** | (base default) | default | default | default | `bedrock/stream_decoder.rb` (AWS binary) |
| **models** | `openai/models.rb` | `anthropic/models.rb` | `google/models.rb` | `ollama/models.rb` | `bedrock/models.rb` |
| **extras** | `responses,images,audio,files,moderations,vector_stores` | `utils.rb, files.rb` | `utils.rb, images.rb, audio.rb, files.rb` | — | `signature.rb` |

Wiring: each provider class lists its `require_relative`s (e.g. `openai.rb:17-27`, `anthropic.rb:17-23`, `bedrock.rb:34-43`), does `include RequestAdapter`, and binds the others via private accessors `stream_parser`/`error_handler`/`stream_decoder` (e.g. `openai.rb:202-208`). `RequestAdapter#adapt` is invoked in `build_complete_request`; `ResponseAdapter.adapt` in `complete`. **DeepSeek** owns only a `request_adapter` (`deepseek/request_adapter.rb`); **XAI/ZAI/LlamaCpp** own *no* request/response/stream/error components — fully inherited from OpenAI (XAI adds `xai/images.rb`).

---

## Step 5 — Streaming & error paths

**Streaming:** `execute` wraps the stream as `LLM::Object.from(streamer:, parser: stream_parser, decoder: stream_decoder)` — `transport/execution.rb:32`. The provider's `stream_parser` (returned by the private accessor, e.g. `openai.rb:202`) parses chunks incrementally: `StreamParser#parse!` merges deltas into `@body` (`openai/stream_parser.rb:29-31,42-59`), with content/tool-call callbacks. Per provider: `{openai,anthropic,google,ollama,bedrock}/stream_parser.rb`. Bedrock additionally overrides `stream_decoder` → `bedrock/stream_decoder.rb` (AWS Event Stream binary framing) vs. the SSE default `LLM::Transport::StreamDecoder` (`provider.rb:391-393`). The OpenAI subclasses (DeepSeek/XAI/ZAI/LlamaCpp) inherit `LLM::OpenAI::StreamParser`.

**Errors:** `handle_response` calls `error_handler.new(tracer, span, res).raise_error!` on non-OK (`transport/execution.rb:51-54`). Each `<provider>/error_handler.rb` maps HTTP status/body → typed errors. Shared error types in `lib/llm/error.rb`: base `Error` `:6`; `UnauthorizedError` `:28`, `RateLimitError` `:32`, `ServerError` `:36`, `NotFoundError` `:40`, `FormatError` `:44`, `PromptError` `:48`, `InvalidRequestError` `:52`, `ContextWindowError` `:56`, plus tool/registry/interrupt errors. Example mapping: `openai/error_handler.rb:51-82` (`error`/`handle_invalid_request`). OpenAI subclasses inherit `LLM::OpenAI::ErrorHandler`.

---

## Step 6 — Every site that must change for a new provider

Assuming a new OpenAI-compatible provider `Foo` (`LLM::Foo < LLM::OpenAI`), modeled on `deepseek`:

**Create:**
- `lib/llm/providers/foo.rb` — the provider class.
- `lib/llm/providers/foo/` components only where behavior differs (e.g. `foo/request_adapter.rb` if request shaping differs, like DeepSeek; otherwise none).
- `data/foo.json` — model registry metadata (`registry.rb:18-25` loads `data/<name>.json`).
- `spec/foo/` — `chat_spec.rb`, `models_spec.rb`, (`request_adapter_spec.rb` if it has one), mirroring `spec/deepseek/`.
- `spec/fixtures/cassettes/foo/...` — VCR cassettes for the specs.

**Change:**
- `lib/llm.rb:~178` — add a `def foo(**)` factory method (lazy require + `LLM::Foo.new`).
- `.env.sample` — add `FOO_SECRET=`.
- `spec/setup.rb:28-37` — add `config.filter_sensitive_data("TOKEN") { ENV["FOO_SECRET"] }`.
- `spec/context_spec.rb:140` — add a `"when given foo"` context (provider + model).
- `spec/registry_spec.rb:55` — add a `"when given foo"` context with `model exists` examples.
- `Rakefile` — `remotes` list (`:7`), console block (`:52`), and `models.dev` provider list (`:72-73`).
- `README.md:28` — provider prose list.
- `CHANGELOG.md` — changelog entry.
- (`llm.gemspec:40` already ships `data/*.json` via glob — no change, but the new JSON must exist.)

Depends-on/reused base: `LLM::OpenAI` (`lib/llm/providers/openai.rb`) and `LLM::Provider` (`lib/llm/provider.rb`) — no edit, just subclassed.

---

## Step 7 — Edit map (for the PR author)

**New files:**
1. `lib/llm/providers/foo.rb` — `class Foo < OpenAI`; `require_relative "openai" unless defined?(LLM::OpenAI)` at top (cf. `deepseek.rb:3`); implement `name` (`:foo`), `initialize(host: "api.foo.com", **)`, `default_model`; disable unsupported APIs with `raise NotImplementedError` (cf. `deepseek.rb:38-72`). Add own request adapter only if needed.
2. *(conditional)* `lib/llm/providers/foo/request_adapter.rb` + `foo/request_adapter/completion.rb` — only if request body differs (pattern: `deepseek/request_adapter.rb:1-27`).
3. `data/foo.json` — model registry (shape per `data/deepseek.json`).
4. `spec/foo/chat_spec.rb` — `LLM.foo(key:)` + `include_examples "LLM::Context: completions"/"text stream"/"tool stream"/"functions"` (copy `spec/deepseek/chat_spec.rb:1-21`).
5. `spec/foo/models_spec.rb` — `default_model` + models list (copy `spec/deepseek/models_spec.rb`).
6. *(conditional)* `spec/foo/request_adapter_spec.rb`.
7. `spec/fixtures/cassettes/foo/chat/*.yml`, `spec/fixtures/cassettes/foo/models/successful_list.yml`.

**Edits:**
- `lib/llm.rb:181` — insert factory `def foo(**) ... LLM::Foo.new(**) end` (pattern `:144-147`).
- `.env.sample:6` — `FOO_SECRET=`.
- `spec/setup.rb:33` — add `filter_sensitive_data("TOKEN") { ENV["FOO_SECRET"] }`.
- `spec/context_spec.rb:140` — new `context "when given foo"` block (provider `LLM.foo(key: "test")`, model).
- `spec/registry_spec.rb:55` — new `context "when given foo"` with `include_examples "model exists", "<model>"`.
- `Rakefile:7,52,72-73` — add `foo` to `remotes`, console, and `models.dev` provider map.
- `README.md:28` and `CHANGELOG.md` — mention the provider.

---

- **Google** (`google.rb:20`) — overrides `user_role/system_role/developer_role` all to `:user` (`:154-170`), `assistant_role` `"model"`; key in URL query; `complete` returns `model` separately (`:77-85`).
- **Ollama** (`ollama.rb:18`) — `embed`+`complete`; streaming on by default (`:121`); `format: schema` instead of response_format.
- **Bedrock** (`bedrock.rb:33`) — AWS SigV4 instead of API key (`initialize` takes `access_key_id/secret_access_key/region`, `:58-70`); `sign!` `:263-270`; Converse payload builder `:233-251`; overrides `stream_decoder` `:200-202`; `tool_role :user`; no `embed/files/images/audio/moderations/responses/vector_stores`.
- **Reuse-by-subclassing of OpenAI:**
  - `DeepSeek` (`deepseek.rb:20`) — `include DeepSeek::RequestAdapter` (own request shaping), reuses OpenAI response/stream/error; disables `files/images/audio/moderations/responses/vector_stores`.
  - `XAI` (`xai.rb:17`) — adds own `images` (`XAI::Images`), disables others.
  - `ZAI` (`zai.rb:16`) — overrides private `completions_path` `:77-79`; disables others.
  - `LlamaCpp` (`llamacpp.rb:22`) — localhost/no-SSL defaults; disables others. Reuses *all* OpenAI components.

---

## Step 4 — Per-provider component families

Each full provider `require_relative`s its components at the top of its class. Families:

| Component | OpenAI | Anthropic | Google | Ollama | Bedrock |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **RequestAdapter** (`adapt`, `adapt_tools`, `adapt_schema`) | `openai/request_adapter.rb` + `request_adapter/{completion,respond,moderation}.rb` | `anthropic/request_adapter.rb` + `request_adapter/completion.rb` | `google/request_adapter.rb` + `request_adapter/*` | `ollama/request_adapter.rb` (+ completion) | `bedrock/request_adapter.rb` + `request_adapter/completion.rb` |
| **ResponseAdapter** (`adapt`/`select`) | `openai/response_adapter.rb` + 10 sub-adapters (`audio,completion,embedding,enumerable,file,image,models,moderations,responds,web_search`) | `anthropic/response_adapter.rb` + subs | `google/response_adapter.rb` + subs | `ollama/response_adapter.rb` | `bedrock/response_adapter.rb` + `response_adapter/{completion,models}.rb` |
| **error_handler** | `openai/error_handler.rb` | `anthropic/error_handler.rb` | `google/error_handler.rb` | `ollama/error_handler.rb` | `bedrock/error_handler.rb` |
| **stream_parser** | `openai/stream_parser.rb` | `anthropic/stream_parser.rb` | `google/stream_parser.rb` | `ollama/stream_parser.rb` | `bedrock/stream_parser.rb` |
| **stream_decoder** | (base default) | default | default | default | `bedrock/stream_decoder.rb` (AWS binary) |
| **models** | `openai/models.rb` | `anthropic/models.rb` | `google/models.rb` | `ollama/models.rb` | `bedrock/models.rb` |
| **extras** | `responses,images,audio,files,moderations,vector_stores` | `utils.rb, files.rb` | `utils.rb, images.rb, audio.rb, files.rb` | — | `signature.rb` |

Wiring: each provider class lists its `require_relative`s (e.g. `openai.rb:17-27`, `anthropic.rb:17-23`, `bedrock.rb:34-43`), does `include RequestAdapter`, and binds the others via private accessors `stream_parser`/`error_handler`/`stream_decoder` (e.g. `openai.rb:202-208`). `RequestAdapter#adapt` is invoked in `build_complete_request`; `ResponseAdapter.adapt` in `complete`. **DeepSeek** owns only a `request_adapter` (`deepseek/request_adapter.rb`); **XAI/ZAI/LlamaCpp** own *no* request/response/stream/error components — fully inherited from OpenAI (XAI adds `xai/images.rb`).

---

## Step 5 — Streaming & error paths

**Streaming:** `execute` wraps the stream as `LLM::Object.from(streamer:, parser: stream_parser, decoder: stream_decoder)` — `transport/execution.rb:32`. The provider's `stream_parser` (returned by the private accessor, e.g. `openai.rb:202`) parses chunks incrementally: `StreamParser#parse!` merges deltas into `@body` (`openai/stream_parser.rb:29-31,42-59`), with content/tool-call callbacks. Per provider: `{openai,anthropic,google,ollama,bedrock}/stream_parser.rb`. Bedrock additionally overrides `stream_decoder` → `bedrock/stream_decoder.rb` (AWS Event Stream binary framing) vs. the SSE default `LLM::Transport::StreamDecoder` (`provider.rb:391-393`). The OpenAI subclasses (DeepSeek/XAI/ZAI/LlamaCpp) inherit `LLM::OpenAI::StreamParser`.

**Errors:** `handle_response` calls `error_handler.new(tracer, span, res).raise_error!` on non-OK (`transport/execution.rb:51-54`). Each `<provider>/error_handler.rb` maps HTTP status/body → typed errors. Shared error types in `lib/llm/error.rb`: base `Error` `:6`; `UnauthorizedError` `:28`, `RateLimitError` `:32`, `ServerError` `:36`, `NotFoundError` `:40`, `FormatError` `:44`, `PromptError` `:48`, `InvalidRequestError` `:52`, `ContextWindowError` `:56`, plus tool/registry/interrupt errors. Example mapping: `openai/error_handler.rb:51-82` (`error`/`handle_invalid_request`). OpenAI subclasses inherit `LLM::OpenAI::ErrorHandler`.

---

## Step 6 — Every site that must change for a new provider

Assuming a new OpenAI-compatible provider `Foo` (`LLM::Foo < LLM::OpenAI`), modeled on `deepseek`:

**Create:**
- `lib/llm/providers/foo.rb` — the provider class.
- `lib/llm/providers/foo/` components only where behavior differs (e.g. `foo/request_adapter.rb` if request shaping differs, like DeepSeek; otherwise none).
- `data/foo.json` — model registry metadata (`registry.rb:18-25` loads `data/<name>.json`).
- `spec/foo/` — `chat_spec.rb`, `models_spec.rb`, (`request_adapter_spec.rb` if it has one), mirroring `spec/deepseek/`.
- `spec/fixtures/cassettes/foo/...` — VCR cassettes for the specs.

**Change:**
- `lib/llm.rb:~178` — add a `def foo(**)` factory method (lazy require + `LLM::Foo.new`).
- `.env.sample` — add `FOO_SECRET=`.
- `spec/setup.rb:28-37` — add `config.filter_sensitive_data("TOKEN") { ENV["FOO_SECRET"] }`.
- `spec/context_spec.rb:140` — add a `"when given foo"` context (provider + model).
- `spec/registry_spec.rb:55` — add a `"when given foo"` context with `model exists` examples.
- `Rakefile` — `remotes` list (`:7`), console block (`:52`), and `models.dev` provider list (`:72-73`).
- `README.md:28` — provider prose list.
- `CHANGELOG.md` — changelog entry.
- (`llm.gemspec:40` already ships `data/*.json` via glob — no change, but the new JSON must exist.)

Depends-on/reused base: `LLM::OpenAI` (`lib/llm/providers/openai.rb`) and `LLM::Provider` (`lib/llm/provider.rb`) — no edit, just subclassed.
